ensemble.other_hypothesis: Transplant kidney pathology (rejection, pyelonephritis, vascular compromise, ureteral obstruction/hydronephrosis); gynecologic (complex right adnexal cyst, ovarian torsion, tubo-ovarian abscess); sepsis from urinary source
ensemble.information_gap: The CT from the outside hospital shows an enlarged and edematous transplant kidney alongside complex intrapelvic free fluid and a large right adnexal cyst, but was done with PO contrast only — it does not provide adequate vascular or parenchymal detail of the transplant kidney; the physician needs to know whether the transplant kidney has adequate perfusion, signs of rejection, hydronephrosis, or perinephric abscess that would explain the fever, hemodynamic instability, and graft-site tenderness.
ensemble.expected_finding: Doppler flow assessment of the transplant kidney showing either preserved or absent/diminished arterial and venous flow, cortical echogenicity changes suggesting rejection or pyelonephritis, collecting system dilation indicating obstruction, or perinephric fluid collection consistent with abscess or urinoma.
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: The transplant kidney is directly tender on exam, appears enlarged and edematous on outside CT, and the patient is febrile with hemodynamic instability and a left-shifted leukocytosis — a dedicated renal transplant ultrasound with Doppler is the standard-of-care next step to evaluate graft perfusion, obstruction, and parenchymal integrity that CT without IV contrast cannot provide.
grounding: Physical examination: 'TTP over graft and lower quadrants'
CT OSH: 'Enlarged and edematous transplant kidney'
History: 's/p 3 kidney transplants, the most recent in ___ of last year'
History: 'Her first two transplants failed due to humoral rejection'
History: 'recurrent UTI/pyelonephritis since her recent transplant and is on chronic suppressive treatment with bactrim'
CT OSH: 'CT OSH with PO contrast' — no IV contrast, limiting vascular assessment
ensemble.reasoning: This immunosuppressed patient with a recent kidney transplant presents with graft-site tenderness, fever, hemodynamic instability, and a leukocytosis with 26% bands; the outside CT (PO contrast only) shows an enlarged, edematous transplant kidney but cannot assess perfusion or collecting system obstruction. A dedicated renal transplant ultrasound with Doppler is the most direct, radiation-free, bedside-available test to evaluate whether the graft has adequate blood flow, is obstructed, or harbors a perinephric collection — any of which would be immediately actionable and could explain the severity of her presentation independent of the appendicitis finding.

vindication.actual_finding: The transplant kidney is normal in size with no hydronephrosis, no perinephric abscess (only a small amount of fluid at the upper pole), and Doppler shows normal arterial waveforms with preserved diastolic flow and resistive indices in the normal range (0.60–0.70).
vindication.relation_to_expectation: The study directly addressed the information gap by providing Doppler flow assessment and parenchymal evaluation, yielding the specific categories of findings the physician anticipated (flow status, collecting system dilation, perinephric fluid), though the results were largely reassuring rather than pathological.
